key_insight: The paper replaces the usual ODE view of linear TD(0) with an SDE approximation that captures both mean contraction and stochastic error-floor behavior under Markovian sampling.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more realistic model for tuning constant-stepsize TD in reinforcement learning, especially when sampling noise is temporally correlated.
method: Derive a diffusion approximation for linear TD(0) with Markovian noise.
Separate projected Bellman contraction dynamics from sampling-induced fluctuations.
Link the steady-state error floor to long-run covariance and operator geometry.
evidence_signal: The abstract presents a theoretical approximation result and interpretation, not a new empirical benchmark or dataset.
watch_item: The approximation may depend on assumptions about linear features, Markovian mixing, and constant stepsize, so it may not directly transfer to nonlinear or highly nonstationary settings.
paper_title: A Diffusion Approximation for Temporal-Difference Learning with Linear Features under Markovian Noise
